title: Linear Equations Basics
subject: Mathematics
text: A linear equation has the form ax + b = c. To solve, isolate x by reversing operations: subtract b from both sides, then divide by a. Always do the same operation to both sides.

title: Common Mistakes in Linear Equations
subject: Mathematics
text: Common mistakes: forgetting to divide all terms by the coefficient, changing signs incorrectly, or moving a term without subtracting. Keep equations balanced.

title: Quadratic Functions Overview
subject: Mathematics
text: A quadratic function is f(x)=ax^2+bx+c. The graph is a parabola. The vertex is at x = -b/(2a). The discriminant b^2-4ac shows the number of real solutions.

title: Factoring Quadratics
subject: Mathematics
text: To factor x^2+bx+c, find two numbers that multiply to c and add to b. Then write (x+m)(x+n). If not factorable, use the quadratic formula.

title: Kinematics Equations
subject: Physics
text: Key equations: v = u + at, s = ut + 1/2 at^2, v^2 = u^2 + 2as. Use consistent units. Identify known variables before solving.

title: Graphs in Kinematics
subject: Physics
text: Slope of a displacement-time graph is velocity. Slope of a velocity-time graph is acceleration. Area under velocity-time graph gives displacement.

title: Ohm's Law
subject: Physics
text: Ohm's Law: V = IR. Current is the rate of flow of charge. Resistance opposes current. In series circuits, resistances add. In parallel, 1/Rt = 1/R1 + 1/R2 + ...

title: Power in Circuits
subject: Physics
text: Electrical power P = VI. Using Ohm's law, P = I^2 R = V^2 / R. Power is in watts.
